notes: Evening-service gravity deck. The bar is open, the dining room is at capacity, the kitchen is screaming. These cards stack the room against you in ways daytime can't — drunken regulars, comp requests, the bar fight you might prevent, the table that won't sit down. 16 cards drawn one per Shadow.

cards.title: THE BAR ROARS
cards.flavor: A laugh cuts the room. Then another. Then it's just the bar all the way to the river window, drowning the cooking line.

cards.title: TWO OF EVERYTHING
cards.flavor: Same order, twice, two tickets, neither fired yet. The cook is laughing about it. The cook is also angry about it.

cards.title: THE WALKER
cards.flavor: A two-top at booth 6 stood up and left without their check. Nobody saw. The bus kid is wiping a different booth.

cards.title: THE PHONE WON'T STOP RINGING
cards.flavor: Reservations. To-gos. A wrong number. A guy who wants to know if you're hiring. The phone is on its eleventh ring.

cards.title: THE JUKEBOX SKIPS
cards.flavor: Same six seconds of a song you've heard a thousand times. Then again. Then again. Somebody whacks the side of it.

cards.title: THE FIGHT WE DIDN'T HAVE
cards.flavor: Two regulars at the bar are leaning at each other in a way that hasn't become anything yet but could. The whole room is half-watching them.

cards.title: THE GRILL IS IN THE WEEDS
cards.flavor: Five tickets up. The cook is calling for you. The line cook helper is keeping pace but only just.

cards.title: A DRINK ON A CHECK
cards.flavor: Bar stool 2 elbows their pint over the ticket. You're going to print it again.

cards.title: THE BIRTHDAY (×30)
cards.flavor: Booth 4: a thirtieth, big party. They want pictures. They want the cake done special. They want everything at once.

cards.title: THE BATHROOM
cards.flavor: Somebody's been in there for a while. Two somebodys are waiting for it. Nobody is going to be okay about this.

cards.title: THE PAYPHONE RINGS
cards.flavor: Pay phone in the back. Long ring. Long. Long. Whoever picks up is going to wish they hadn't.

cards.title: THE WINDOW ARGUES
cards.flavor: The river window: someone outside is hitting the glass. They want in. They are not a customer.

cards.title: IN THE WEEDS
cards.flavor: The kitchen is shouting. The bar is shouting. The dining room is shouting. The bus kid is laughing in the bus tub.

cards.title: EIGHTY-SIX THE SPECIAL
cards.flavor: The kitchen is out of the thing the menu has been pushing all night. Half your tables ordered it.

cards.title: THE LIGHTS
cards.flavor: Half the bar's bulbs go out at once. The fluorescent over the grill steadies. The room narrows to the warm part.

cards.title: CLOSING TIME
cards.flavor: The hostess flips the sign. Last call. Some tables don't notice. The room is half-empty in five minutes and you can hear the cooler hum again.
